question: Will either Mpox clade 1b or West Nile Virus be detected in U.S. wastewater on August 1, 2026?

factors.description: WNV is strongly seasonal peaking in July-August; Aug 1 is near peak season with ~140 sites monitored nationwide and per-site peak positivity of 3-13%. At-least-one positive is near-certain.
Resolution phrasing 'any samples positive for the period through the reporting date' favors a cumulative reading; WNV already had positives in Dec 2025 and May 2026.
Two independent OR paths (WNV or Mpox clade Ib) both provide a route to YES.
Downside: dashboard may show a narrow rolling window or national aggregate reading 'not-detected', or accessibility issues at resolution (mitigated by backup source).

summary: The question resolves YES if the WastewaterSCAN dashboard shows any positive samples for Mpox clade 1b OR West Nile Virus through the reporting date on/after Aug 1, 2026. August 1 is near the peak of WNV season, ~140 sites are monitored nationwide, and a retrospective study showed per-site positivity of 3-13% during the summer/fall peak. Even one positive sample suffices, and Mpox clade Ib offers a second path. The 'any samples positive for the period through the reporting date' phrasing also favors a cumulative reading, under which WNV has already had detections (Dec 2025, May 2026). The main residual risk is technical—dashboard display window or accessibility—partly mitigated by the fine-print backup source. Final estimate ~0.97.
reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.97 (YES)**

West Nile Virus detection in US wastewater around August 1, 2026 is nearly certain. Key reasoning:

- **Peak season timing**: August 1 sits at the peak of WNV season. With ~140 sites monitored nationwide and per-site positivity of 3–13% during the summer/fall peak (per a retrospective study), the probability that at least one site shows a positive is very high.
- **Low bar**: Only a single positive sample is needed to resolve YES.
- **Two independent paths**: Either WNV or Mpox clade Ib detection triggers YES.
- **Cumulative phrasing**: "Any samples positive...for the period through the reporting date" favors an any-positive reading; WNV already had detections in Dec 2025 and May 2026.

The main residual risk is technical (dashboard display window or accessibility), partly mitigated by the fine-print backup source—which keeps this just short of certainty at 0.97.
